reason: Response B has a more direct, efficient prose style that moves through ideas in a single flowing paragraph without dramatic structural flourishes, which feels more characteristic of Sonnet's tendency toward clear, well-organized but less ornate writing. Response A uses a more literary, layered approach with distinct paragraphs building on each other and a more elaborate philosophical observation ("a sound made largely of what isn't there"), which feels more like Opus's deeper, more expansive reasoning style. Both are high quality, but B's concise single-paragraph treatment and its practical framing (sleep, focused work) feel more like my own approach.
